hero.label: WEB DEVELOPER • WEB ACCESSIBILITY SPECIALIST
hero.title: Webs that work for everyone.
hero.title_1: No fines, no excuses, no smoke.
hero.subtitle: I don't make pretty websites. I understand your problem, choose the right tools and connect them so your business works better.

context.title: Web accessibility is no longer optional in Spain.
context.subtitle: Since June 28, 2025, Law 11/2023 requires thousands of companies to make their websites accessible. Almost none of them are.

stats.description: of Spanish websites fail to meet the regulation.
people with disabilities in Spain cannot use many websites.

context.closing: If you sell online, take bookings, process services or receive public funds, this is about you.
context.sources: Sources: 2025 Web Accessibility Barometer, INE, RDL 1/2013.

about.label: ABOUT ME
about.title: I was solving problems before I knew how to code
about.text_1: In the factory, I was already doing the same thing I do now: looking at a process, finding what doesn't work and imagining how to make it better. The difference is that before I thought about it and now I build it.
about.text_2: I studied Engineering in Topography — a career of measuring, calculating and being precise. I worked as a laborer in factories — where you see how things really work, not just the theory. And one day I discovered that web development allowed me to combine both things: technical precision and practical problem-solving.
about.text_3: Today my focus is on web accessibility. Laws have changed, websites haven't. There are thousands of companies required to comply with regulations that almost no one understands, with serious fines and real deadlines. And that's exactly the type of problem I've been solving my whole life: seeing how something should work and making it work.
about.cta: If your website is on that list, let's talk.

approach.label: APPROACH
approach.title: Three steps. No surprises, no locked-in subscriptions, no smoke.
approach.subtitle: Every project follows the same process. You know it before we start and you know exactly where we are at any moment.

steps.title: Audit
steps.description: I review your site against WCAG 2.1 AA and Spain's Law 11/2023. I combine technical tools (axe, Lighthouse, screen readers) with manual evaluation. It's not a 200-page PDF full of jargon: I deliver a report anyone on your team can understand, with each issue, its impact, and how much it costs to fix.

steps.title: Fix
steps.description: I take the report and apply it. Me — not an outsourced team on the other side of the world. Every fix is tested with a keyboard, with a screen reader, and with real users when needed. When we're done, I hand you an accessibility statement ready to publish.

steps.title: Maintain (optional)
steps.description: Websites change. Plugins get updated, the marketing team uploads content, developers ship deploys. If you want to sleep at night, I review your site every month and keep accessibility up to date. No annual contract — leave whenever you want.

items.title: Smart tax assistant
items.description: A chat where you ask your tax questions and get answers based on real tax authority documentation. Like having a tax advisor available around the clock.

items.title: Design Systems Generator
items.description: You describe your brand — colors, style, personality — and it generates a complete visual guide with every element your website needs. Ready to use.

items.title: Automated web audit
items.description: You enter a website address and it tells you what's working well, what's running slow, and what needs fixing. All explained in plain language, not cryptic metrics.

items.title: AI study platform - In Testing
items.description: A study assistant that helps you prepare for public exams. It quizzes you, explains what you don't understand, and adapts to your level so you study smarter, not harder.

contact.label: CONTACT
contact.title: Does your website comply with Law 11/2023?
contact.subtitle: If you're not sure, it probably doesn't. Tell me what you have and I'll tell you where you stand. No strings attached, no PowerPoints, no smoke.
contact.note: I get back to you in under 24 hours. Business days.

notFound.subtitle: But at least I'm telling you straight, no excuses. Maybe the address is wrong, or maybe it's a page that's no longer here.

hero.titlePrefix: Complete audit of your website to comply with
hero.subtitle: In 5–7 business days you get a report your whole team can understand, with every issue, its legal impact, and how much it costs to fix. No jargon, no 200-page PDFs, no smoke.
hero.priceNote: For websites with up to 8 unique pages.
hero.priceSubnote: Larger sites: fixed quote in 24 h, no surprises.

problem.title: Not optional. Not for 2030. Right now.
problem.subtitle: Since June 28, 2025, Spain's Law 11/2023 requires thousands of companies to make their websites accessible. The vast majority haven't. And this is not a small problem.

risks.title: Real fines
risks.description: The sanctioning regime of RDL 1/2013 is already in force. Spanish regions can inspect. Disability associations are already filing real cases.

risks.title: Locked out of public tenders
risks.description: If you work or want to work with the Spanish public sector, it's no longer optional. RD 193/2023 applies to every supplier since this date.

risks.title: Customers who can't use your site
risks.description: People with disabilities in Spain. If your ecommerce, your booking system or your form fails with a screen reader, those customers go to your competition.

problem.closing: Auditing your website is not a compliance whim. It's what separates the companies that are ready from those who will end up with the fine, the lawsuit, or both.

deliverables.title: A clear report. A closed roadmap. Zero jargon.
deliverables.subtitle: This is what you receive when the audit is done, in 5–7 business days, in a single document anyone on your team can read.

items.title: Executive summary
items.description: A 2-page summary with your site's real compliance level, the specific legal risk you carry today, and the priorities to resolve it. Designed for management to understand without being technical.

items.title: Catalog of findings by WCAG criterion
items.description: Every issue documented with screenshot, violated WCAG criterion, severity level, and affected page or component. No gratuitous jargon, enough detail for your tech team to act.

items.title: Prioritized remediation plan
items.description: Every finding with its concrete solution, estimated effort in hours, and the optimal order to resolve it. You know what to fix first, how long it takes, and how much it costs.

items.title: Accessibility statement ready to publish
items.description: Legal document written under Spain's RD 1112/2018 with your real compliance level. You publish it on your site and start meeting the formal requirement from day one.

items.title: Results presentation meeting
items.description: A one-hour video call to review the report with you and your team, answer questions and prioritize actions. I don't send a PDF and disappear.

whoMustComply.title: Does this apply to you?
whoMustComply.subtitle: Not all websites are obligated equally. Spain's Law 11/2023 and RD 193/2023 define three groups with different deadlines. These are.

groups.headline: If you sell online or run digital services for consumers, you are already obligated.
groups.body: Ecommerce of any size. Online banking. Hotels with online booking. Airlines, rail, sea carriers. Telecom operators. Streaming platforms. Electricity, gas and water utilities. Online education platforms. Online travel agencies and tour operators.
groups.note: Exception: micro-enterprises (under 10 employees AND under €2M revenue) that only provide services without product sales.

groups.headline: If your sector runs a public-facing website, you're up in 2029.
groups.body: Clinics and healthcare services with online booking. Restaurants with online orders or bookings. Academies and training. Cultural, sports companies and museums with online sales. Mid-sized real estate. Financial services and insurance.
groups.note: Getting ahead now is a competitive advantage: most of your sector will only comply at the last minute.

groups.headline: If you receive public funds, the obligation is now, with no grace period.
groups.body: Spain's Kit Digital, EU funds, regional grants, contracts with the public sector. The Accessibility Review Report (IRA) is already required. Without compliance, risk of fund refund.

whoMustComply.closing: If you don't fit any of the three groups, you probably don't need this audit right now. If you fit at least one, you do.

process.title: 5 phases. 7 days. Zero improvisation.
process.subtitle: From the moment you confirm the audit to the moment you get the report in your inbox, this is the process. You know at every step where we are and what I need from you.

steps.title: Briefing and access
steps.description: A short call to understand your site: type of business, unique templates, integrations, private areas if any, accesses I'll need. If there's something specific you're worried about, we mark it as a priority focus.
steps.clientInput: What I need from you: 30 minutes on a video call and access credentials if applicable.

steps.title: Automated analysis and mapping
steps.description: I run tools (axe, Lighthouse, Pa11y) on the selected templates. I map the site's structure, identify critical components and flows to evaluate. I generate the first inventory of automated findings.
steps.clientInput: What I need from you: nothing. I work, you keep on with your stuff.

steps.title: Manual and technical evaluation
steps.description: 70% of the real work. Keyboard navigation, screen reader checks with NVDA and VoiceOver, color contrast validation, focus visibility, forms, ARIA, error messages. Every finding documented with screenshot, WCAG criterion and impact level.
steps.clientInput: What I need from you: nothing. If something critical comes up, I'll let you know right away.

steps.title: Report writing and remediation plan
steps.description: I structure all findings in the final document: executive summary, catalog by criterion, prioritized plan with estimated hours, accessibility statement. I review twice before delivering.
steps.clientInput: What I need from you: nothing. What I need from myself: rigor.

steps.title: Delivery and presentation meeting
steps.description: I send you the full report. We schedule a 1-hour video call to review it with you and your team, prioritize actions and resolve doubts. From here, you decide: you apply it yourself, we apply it together, or we continue with the remediation plan.
steps.clientInput: What I need from you: 1 hour on a video call and the team involved.

afterAudit.title: The audit is the beginning, not the end.
afterAudit.subtitle: Once you have the report, you decide. These are the two ways I can keep working with you if you need it.

options.title: Apply the corrections
options.description: If you want me to implement the solutions from the report, we can do that. I work package by package by priority: critical first, accessory later. Each package with a closed scope, estimated hours and fixed price before starting.
options.priceNote: Typical investment: between €1,800 and €6,000, depending on the volume of findings. You know for sure at the end of the audit, never before.

options.title: Keep it from breaking again
options.description: Websites change. Plugins update, marketing uploads content, devs ship deploys. If you want to sleep at night, I review your site every month and keep compliance up to date. The accessibility statement gets updated, new findings get fixed.
options.priceNote: From €149/month. No annual contract, you leave when you want.

afterAudit.closing: Neither of the two is mandatory. The audit alone, with the report in hand, already meets the legal requirement of evaluation. The rest is up to you.

whyMe.title: No team. Just me. That's why it works.
whyMe.subtitle: I don't sell you a corporate process. I sell you the work of one professional who takes responsibility for what they deliver.

reasons.title: I audit, I fix, I answer
reasons.description: There's no junior taking the data, account manager presenting it and external developer implementing it. You talk to me at every stage. The person signing the report is the same one writing it and the same one picking up the phone if something doesn't fit.

reasons.title: I know the law, not just the tech
reasons.description: Any developer with a script can find problems. Few know what exactly Spain's RD 193/2023 requires, what exceptions Law 11/2023 applies or how the accessibility statement is drafted. I spend weeks studying the legal framework before applying it to your site.

reasons.title: Closed price, closed scope, closed deadline
reasons.description: No 'estimate subject to scope'. No 'depends on scope'. No surprise invoices. What you see on this page is what you pay, no more, no less. If I discover your case needs more, I tell you before, never after.

faq.title: What people usually ask before hiring.

items.question: How long does the audit take exactly?
items.answer: 5-7 business days from the initial briefing. Most audits I deliver in 5. Larger sites can require up to 7.

items.question: What if my site has more than 8 unique pages?
items.answer: I send you a fixed quote in 24 hours based on the real volume. No surprises, no negotiation. Tiers go by size brackets, not by hour worked.

items.question: Does the audit include implementing the corrections?
items.answer: No. The audit is only the diagnosis: I deliver the full report with every finding and the remediation plan. Implementation is optional, with a separate quote, and only if you decide I do it.

items.question: Do I need anything ready before starting?
items.answer: Just access to your site (if it has private areas or staging). If you don't have any of that, even better: the audit is faster.

items.question: Can I apply the corrections myself?
items.answer: Yes. The report is designed so your technical team can act on it directly. If you have an in-house developer or agency, you hand them the document and they apply it.

items.question: Does this fulfill the legal requirement of Law 11/2023?
items.answer: Yes. The audit includes the accessibility statement drafted under RD 1112/2018, ready to publish on your site. It meets the formal evaluation requirement of the regulation.

items.question: How is it invoiced?
items.answer: 50% at confirmation of the audit start, 50% at report delivery. Formal invoice with VAT, valid for deduction.

items.question: What if your audit misses something and I get reported later?
items.answer: I work with WCAG 2.1 AA + EN 301549 methodology. It's the legal standard in Spain. If a finding slips and appears later, I fix it at no additional cost within 60 days after report delivery.

priceAndDeadline.closing: If it fits, we talk. If it doesn't fit, I tell you that too.
